title: Day 35: El Jardinero Confirmed as Primary CJNG Target; Tío Lako Emerges as Fourth Succession Candidate
detail: Day 35 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). With El Doble R in custody, El Tuli dead, and O3 consolidating under a collective council structure, U.S. and Mexican intelligence services have confirmed El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) as the primary remaining CJNG targeting priority — with a $5 million U.S. State Department reward outstanding. A fourth succession candidate, Heraclio Guerrero Martínez (alias 'Tío Lako' / 'Uncle Lako'), has been identified in U.S. intelligence assessments alongside O3, El Jardinero, and El Sapo (whose status remains unconfirmed) as a senior CJNG figure with logistics and financial oversight roles. Tío Lako has historically managed CJNG corridor operations in northern and central Mexico, and his profile has risen as competing succession candidates have been arrested or confirmed dead. DEA and HSI liaisons in Guadalajara continue high-tempo operations under the Joint Interagency Task Force–Counter Cartel (JITC-CC) framework. No senior-command-level captures have been publicly confirmed today; operations continue with multiple lower-level arrests across Jalisco and Michoacán.

media.caption: El Jardinero confirmed as primary CJNG target; Tio Lako emerges as fourth succession candidate

title: Tequila, Jalisco Mayor Arrested in Operativo Enjambre for Alleged CJNG Ties
detail: The municipal president (mayor) of Tequila, Jalisco — home to Mexico's iconic tequila-producing highlands — was arrested as part of the ongoing Operativo Enjambre sweep targeting corrupt local officials with alleged CJNG connections. The arrest extends a documented pattern of municipal-level institutional capture: earlier in March, five Chavinda, Michoacán officials were detained in the same operation (March 19–20). Charges include extortion, homicide facilitation, and functioning as a CJNG liaison official. The Tequila arrest underscores the cartel's deep penetration of Jalisco's governmental infrastructure — the state at the heart of CJNG's historical operational base. Operativo Enjambre has now resulted in dozens of public official arrests across CJNG's zones since it was intensified after El Mencho's February 22 killing. Security analysts note that dismantling local-government corruption networks is essential to preventing plaza bosses from reconstituting operational capacity following national-level leadership decapitation.

media.caption: Tequila, Jalisco mayor arrested in Operativo Enjambre for alleged CJNG ties
